summary: Machine policy for Windows Connect Now wizards. The local Microsoft WindowsConnectNow.admx file documents the same WCN UI policy key and DisableWcnUi value used by the app.

setting.name: Windows Connect Now Wizards
setting.casual_explanation: Windows Connect Now is an older wizard-based way to help set up some wireless routers and wireless devices. This policy decides whether those wizards are available.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable WCN wizards on managed systems or modern setups where the legacy wizard flow is unnecessary and only adds extra UI surface.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning it off removes old setup wizards, but it also removes the built-in guided flow for some wireless setup tasks.
setting.professional_notes: WindowsConnectNow.admx defines both user and machine versions of DisableWcnUi. The app uses the machine policy at HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WCN\UI\DisableWcnUi with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: The device is not forcing the Windows Connect Now wizard policy.

allowed_values.label: WCN wizards allowed
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled and users can access the Windows Connect Now wizard tasks.

allowed_values.label: WCN wizards blocked
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is enabled and users cannot access the Windows Connect Now wizard tasks.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes DisableWcnUi = 1 at machine scope, which matches the documented blocked state.

windows_defaults.label: Windows machine-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows Vista and newer systems that still expose Windows Connect Now policy definitions

states.rationale: The machine policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: The local ADML help text says users can access all WCN wizards when the policy is disabled or not configured.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Systems that may still use legacy guided wireless setup
avoid_for: Managed environments that do not want WCN wizard surfaces
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to preserve the default wireless setup wizard availability.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to remove legacy WCN wizard entry points.
tradeoffs: Older wireless setup UI stays exposed.

recommended_profiles.label: No WCN wizards
intended_for: Modern deployments that do not use Windows Connect Now
avoid_for: Users who still depend on legacy wireless setup wizards

states.rationale: Turn off the Windows Connect Now wizard tasks through policy.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to remove legacy WCN setup wizards from the system.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You still use those guided wireless setup tasks.
tradeoffs: Users lose access to WCN wizard tasks such as router or wireless-device setup.
Legacy configuration UI surface is reduced.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft WindowsConnectNow.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file defines both user and machine DisableWcnUi policies with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0 at the WCN UI policy path.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft WindowsConnectNow.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text says enabling the policy turns off the Windows Connect Now wizards and disables related tasks such as setting up a wireless router or adding a wireless device.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes DisableWcnUi = 1 at machine scope for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak source record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: The local Microsoft WindowsConnectNow.admx file explicitly defines DisableWcnUi under Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WCN\UI, and the paired ADML help text documents that enabling the policy turns off Windows Connect Now wizard tasks. The app writes that exact blocked state.

validation_proof.notes: The local official Microsoft ADMX file names the same machine policy surface used by the app. The paired ADML help text states that enabling the setting turns off the Windows Connect Now wizards and disables related wizard tasks.
